arguments.task: Research Fathom using official first-party sources only where possible. Open fathom.video and find links to developer/API documentation, authentication documentation, pricing or plans pages, support/help center, changelog/release notes, GitHub or MCP pages if any. Also search the public web for official Fathom API docs and MCP references if not visible on the site. Extract URLs and concise findings about: whether a public API exists, auth methods, whether access is self-serve or gated, pricing/access requirements, rate limits, and whether there is an MCP server or registry listing. Return only evidence-backed findings with source URLs.

input.thought: Launch browser research task for Fathom across official docs and public sources.

raw_result.description: AI meeting assistant for recording, transcribing, summarizing, and sharing calls.
raw_result.auth_notes: Could not verify public API authentication from the gathered evidence. Public product site shows app login and enterprise SSO/SCIM on Enterprise pricing, but no verified developer auth docs were gathered.
raw_result.gating_reason: Pricing page shows a free forever self-serve plan with unlimited recordings; no evidence gathered of API-specific sales gating.

api_surface.breadth: No public developer API documentation or endpoint reference was verified from the gathered evidence.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No MCP server or registry listing was verified from the gathered evidence.

raw_result.blocker: No verified public API documentation or documented API authentication was gathered, so a toolkit cannot be reliably built today from evidence in hand.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: No rate-limit documentation was verified from the gathered evidence.
raw_result.notable_signals: Pricing page indicates Free, Team, Business, and Enterprise tiers; Enterprise includes SSO/SCIM and priority support, suggesting mature enterprise features on the product side.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Several required fields remain unverified from the gathered evidence, especially whether any public API exists, its auth method, rate limits, and whether an MCP server exists. Conclusion is based mainly on the product homepage and pricing page.
